# Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how… | Tristha Ramamurthy | 133 comments

# Tristha Ramamurthy’s Post

[![Image 2: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/D5603AQED5LtwC1QB7A/profile-displayphoto-scale_400_400/B56ZrEOtkUK4Ak-/0/1764228787597?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=1Ah49nAjRx9vDNKvltD6p0qOAcwWPTPIseTv7UjSQ6k)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Ekya Schools•20K followers

 4d 

*   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

*   ![Image 3: No alternative text description for this image](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/D5622AQE4mVJqBGHT-g/feedshare-shrink_1280/B56Z1xbFHGGoAM-/0/1775724417788?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=mK08mcW7LI6k0z2kuuJhSp5YDzgn7MH0swRf8aa5GCo)

 10h 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Totally agree with you mam! Main problem of edtech companies they are focusing only on marketing not education. Sorry to say most of the edtech companies are performing like kapil sharma show; only and only [entertainment.viral](http://entertainment.viral/?trk=public_post_comment-text) funny and sometimes useless derogatory reel are roaming through social media. Change only will come if dedicated professional got chance to expand who teach students in small batches offline and focus on discipline content online.

[Mohammed Zeeshan](https://in.linkedin.com/in/mohammed-zeeshan-359bb368?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Imarticus Learning•14K followers

 4d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Outcomes Drive Trust. Trust Drives Sustainable Growth. Outcomes take time. Trust takes even more time. This is not built for the VC Rocketship Growth Template. It is not a "Consumer Tech" playbook. I remember my first call with one of the "Unicorn" edtechs founding team who wanted to explore M&A with us. They were proud to call themselves an Ecommerce company, operating in ed. I couldnt bear hearing that and I excused myself from the call shortly. Fast forward, and they are now worth 1/10th of their peak valuation, struggling to get acquired :) I saw founders pivot from building in consumer spaces to ed, because it was the flavour of the season, and now see them pivot again to not be willing to be called "ed" anymore. The legacy brands built in Ed, were built through brick and mortar, through growth being driven by strong brand, trust, student love, love for the teacher and consistent outcomes built over decades.

[![Image 9: Shailesh Pyasi, graphic](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/shaileshpyasi?trk=public_post_comment_actor-image)

[Shailesh Pyasi](https://in.linkedin.com/in/shaileshpyasi?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Social Exam Prep!•1K followers

 12h 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

India’s EdTech space is largely driven by scale, not innovation. Most platforms revolve around the same model—apps, video lectures, and test series, primarily targeting competitive exams. The focus is on expansion and visibility rather than improving how students actually learn. What’s missing? Experience over content. Students don’t just need material, they need clarity, experience, and outcomes. Instead of targeted spending on learning outcomes, the industry pours resources into ads and PR. Teachers are turned into “celebrity figures,” and rivalries trend more than real educational value. At the same time, connections often outweigh merit, and aggressive emotional marketing by dominant players is quietly killing innovative startups. EdTech in India has the potential to transform learning but right now, it is chasing short-term metrics over long-term impact. The real shift needed: from scaling content → to delivering outcomes.

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply)[1 Reaction](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reactions) 2 Reactions 

[![Image 10: Kalyan Singh, graphic](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/kalyansingh-education?trk=public_post_comment_actor-image)

[Kalyan Singh](https://in.linkedin.com/in/kalyansingh-education?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Police Modern School,Dehradun•1K followers

 4d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

The gap is not just about patience, it is about alignment between capital and classroom realities. Most investments optimise for scale, visibility, and speed, while real education systems require consistency, behavioural change, and long-term reinforcement. Curriculum design, teacher capability, and assessment reform are not quick-return levers. They demand sustained engagement, governance discipline, and continuous monitoring. Without that, even well-funded solutions remain add-ons rather than becoming part of the core learning system. The real shift will happen when capital moves from funding products to strengthening institutional systems that can consistently deliver outcomes at scale.

 4d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

A lot of money is going into edtech. But not necessarily into education. Most of it is directed towards skill development, test prep, and improving access or visibility. Very little is going into improving education quality or measuring real outcomes.. (alumni quality being a clear indicator) At the core, the system hasn’t changed. Curriculum lags. College admissions still rely on marks and competitive exams… the focus remains on admissions, not student outcomes. Less than 0.5% choose teaching, so there is no real new supply of educators. The funding is not fixing the foundation. It is just building around it

 4d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_comment-text) I agree funding often misses classroom realities. But reading the comments here, I think we’re still being too generous to the system. The uncomfortable truth is, schools themselves aren’t ready for the change they advocate. We talk about innovation, yet operate within rigid timetables, exam pressure, and compliance-driven teaching. Even the best-funded solutions fail in environments that don’t allow teachers to experiment or adapt. The gap isn’t just capital vs classrooms, it’s intention vs execution.

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply)[5 Reactions](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reactions) 6 Reactions

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

The issue is education cannot be equated to a business alone. Yes, it needs to be sustainable but it also needs time to show impact. And that impact eeds to be understood in ways beyond revenue

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply)[5 Reactions](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reactions) 6 Reactions 

[![Image 14: Venubabu Chandra, graphic](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/venubabu-chandra-925b3622?trk=public_post_comment_actor-image)

[Venubabu Chandra](https://in.linkedin.com/in/venubabu-chandra-925b3622?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Sunstone•854 followers

 4d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Fully aligned—this is fundamentally a context gap, not a capital gap. Classrooms are not uniform. Every room carries different intelligence levels, motivations, backgrounds, aspirations, and emotional states. And in K–12, a significant part of learning is non-academic—confidence, curiosity, discipline, belief—areas most systems don’t even attempt to understand. What we often call “gaps” through analytics are just surface signals. They don’t explain why a student disengages, what drives effort, or when learning actually clicks. That layer is deeply contextual—and only one actor consistently reads and responds to it in real time: the teacher. EdTech tends to standardize for scale. Teachers continuously adapt for reality. If capital truly wants impact, it has to move toward systems that respect classroom diversity, go beyond academic metrics, and strengthen teacher-led decisions—rather than bypass them.

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply)[1 Reaction](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reactions) 2 Reactions

[Nishant Kumar](https://in.linkedin.com/in/nishant-kumar-87820612a?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Project DEFY•2K followers

 1d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Strongly resonate with this. From what I have seen, the biggest shift doesn’t come from ‘better content delivery’ but from reimagining the role of the learner itself. When young people take ownership of what and how they learn, classrooms—and even the idea of classrooms—start to change. That kind of shift doesn’t fit neatly into typical investment cycles, which is why it often gets overlooked.

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply)[1 Reaction](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reactions) 2 Reactions

[Christopher S Dhas](https://in.linkedin.com/in/careeros?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name)
Career OS•3K followers

 1d 

*   [Report this comment](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

If you look at any investor pitch, it rarely talks about learning impact. Its more about app downloads, time on platform, course completions and data collected. Real classroom change... how a teacher asks a question, how a child learns to think... no one in boardrooms care about these. The sad truth is that this pattern has now entered mainstream indian education. One only needs to look at the recent boom of Private equity/Venture capital firms entering the private school sector.

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_like)[Reply](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Ftristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV&trk=public_post_comment_reply) 1 Reaction

## More Relevant Posts

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/hemantghatole_education-that-fulfilled-the-genuine-need-activity-7448000354274963456-m5vC)

[![Image 17: View profile for Hemant S Ghatole](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/hemantghatole?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Hemant S Ghatole](https://in.linkedin.com/in/hemantghatole?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
Jetking Thane Centre (…•7K followers

 4d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fhemantghatole_education-that-fulfilled-the-genuine-need-activity-7448000354274963456-m5vC&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Education that fulfilled the genuine need of the society today and in the future at the lowest price and with the highest value . This is what an investor must look for before investing in an edtech . Today AI based education can be the most cost effective ,customizable and result oriented form of education .

[![Image 18: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Founder, Ekya Schools | Advancing meaningful education | Building institutions that endure | Provost, CMR University | Board Advisor | Harvard | King’s College, London | Stanford

 4d 

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

    *   ![Image 19: No alternative text description for this image](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fhemantghatole_education-that-fulfilled-the-genuine-need-activity-7448000354274963456-m5vC&trk=public_post_like-cta)[Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fhemantghatole_education-that-fulfilled-the-genuine-need-activity-7448000354274963456-m5vC&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tanujaa-hemakumar-942755215_as-someone-working-in-design-thinking-and-activity-7448002553226723328-IWSY)

[![Image 20: View profile for Tanujaa Hemakumar](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tanujaa-hemakumar-942755215?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Tanujaa Hemakumar](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tanujaa-hemakumar-942755215?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
Edturn•2K followers

 4d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftanujaa-hemakumar-942755215_as-someone-working-in-design-thinking-and-activity-7448002553226723328-IWSY&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

As someone working in design thinking and innovation, what I see in most edtech startups today is either fancy dashboards for schools, AI-led automation, or better ways to prepare for exams. But none of this truly provokes curiosity or builds real-world awareness in children. Many still grow up like a frog in a well unaware of what’s happening around them and often feel lost when they step into the real world. Two years ago, We asked a simple question: “How can we make a child relevant to today’s world in a way that is fun, curiosity-driven, and helps them explore interests, understand the world, and discover new career paths while preserving their thinking in the AI era?” After extensive research, this led us to design our own thinking framework - Q-DIME, and a community-driven ecosystem to build it as a habit - EdTurn. [www.edturn.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eedturn%2Ecom&urlhash=Sv5a&trk=public_post-text)

[![Image 21: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Founder, Ekya Schools | Advancing meaningful education | Building institutions that endure | Provost, CMR University | Board Advisor | Harvard | King’s College, London | Stanford

 4d 

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftanujaa-hemakumar-942755215_as-someone-working-in-design-thinking-and-activity-7448002553226723328-IWSY&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/dr-radhakrishnaiah-parachuru-0960ba20_not-everyone-who-invests-in-education-and-activity-7448403212165881856-4eC9)

[![Image 24: View profile for Dr. Radhakrishnaiah Parachuru](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dr-radhakrishnaiah-parachuru-0960ba20?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Dr. Radhakrishnaiah Parachuru](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dr-radhakrishnaiah-parachuru-0960ba20?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
American Association of…•8K followers

 3d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdr-radhakrishnaiah-parachuru-0960ba20_not-everyone-who-invests-in-education-and-activity-7448403212165881856-4eC9&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Not everyone who invests in education and educational technologies invests with a commercial motive. There are many Indians living abroad who remember where they came from, what conditions they faced in their childhood, and how they learned in their yearly years. For them, improving learning conditions using some of the learning enhancement tools that proved successful in Western countries is an attractive option and motivation. There are also many within India who became successful after growing up in rural and semi-rural areas and then moved to greener patches for economic reasons. Love for their native localities is the main motivating factor for the money they are willing to spend on educational infrastructure. Saying that people spend money on learning activities to serve their own needs is a dishonor to benevolent donors. Along with investment in the right type of educational infrastructure, what we need is training of teachers and end users on how to take full advantage of each and every new tool that is made available. We need skilled local volunteers to maintain these facilities and make them available to users on daily basis. We need people who do this more for the love of learning & service and less for filling up their pockets. Some of the IT tools that greatly simplify learning can also boost adult education and aid in the timely remediation of agricultural needs such as soil remediation and timely diagnosis of the diseases of people and crops. They can help to effciently convey all local problems to appropriate government and voluntary agencies. Instead of blaming infrastructure investments, let us help create an army of volunteers who can optimize the everyday usage of the proven tools and their maintenance. This can bring in more investments to learning.

[![Image 25: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Founder, Ekya Schools | Advancing meaningful education | Building institutions that endure | Provost, CMR University | Board Advisor | Harvard | King’s College, London | Stanford

 4d 

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

[![Image 28: View profile for Nilesh Khare Ph.D. (Strategy), ICF-PCC](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/nileshkhare?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Nilesh Khare Ph.D. (Strategy), ICF-PCC](https://in.linkedin.com/in/nileshkhare?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
I have worked with borads…•11K followers

 3d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnileshkhare_education-learning-activity-7448588080560656384-8zsW&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

[#Education](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducation&trk=public_post-text)[#Learning](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flearning&trk=public_post-text) Could not agree more! We fund infra, content, and delivery and not the process of learning that needs engagement, facilitation, orientation, and inner motivation. The pursuit of earning a degree/certificate is not the same as pursuit of learning. The real education and learning is a far more complex challenge to address. What do you think?

[![Image 29: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Founder, Ekya Schools | Advancing meaningful education | Building institutions that endure | Provost, CMR University | Board Advisor | Harvard | King’s College, London | Stanford

 4d 

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

[![Image 31](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV) 5](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnileshkhare_education-learning-activity-7448588080560656384-8zsW&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnileshkhare_education-learning-activity-7448588080560656384-8zsW&trk=public_post_like-cta)[Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnileshkhare_education-learning-activity-7448588080560656384-8zsW&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnileshkhare_education-learning-activity-7448588080560656384-8zsW&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/shreyanshdwivedi23_education-doesnt-suffer-from-a-lack-of-funding-activity-7448602457141690368-IeWf)

[![Image 32: View profile for Shreyansh Dwivedi](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/shreyanshdwivedi23?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Shreyansh Dwivedi](https://in.linkedin.com/in/shreyanshdwivedi23?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
Muskaan Dreams•663 followers

 3d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fshreyanshdwivedi23_education-doesnt-suffer-from-a-lack-of-funding-activity-7448602457141690368-IeWf&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Education doesn’t suffer from a lack of funding. And we don’t have an ed-tech shortage. We have a last-mile problem. Education needs direction, momentum, and follow-through. Money flows easily into ideas, platforms, and products. But classrooms don’t run on pitch decks—they run on teachers, consistency, and daily effort. Students sit in rooms full of “infrastructure” that doesn’t translate into learning. The excitement fades faster than it’s created. Classrooms don’t change with devices. They change with people who show up, again and again. Infrastructure creates access. But only effort, patience, and trust create impact. The real gap isn’t innovation. It’s staying long enough to make it work. Because education isn’t a quick win. It’s slow, uncomfortable, and deeply human. But when it works, it doesn’t just change classrooms—it changes lives

[![Image 33: View profile for Tristha Ramamurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Tristha Ramamurthy](https://in.linkedin.com/in/tristha?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Founder, Ekya Schools | Advancing meaningful education | Building institutions that endure | Provost, CMR University | Board Advisor | Harvard | King’s College, London | Stanford

 4d 

Every year, many millions are invested in India’s ed-tech market, yet they bring little impact to real education. I keep asking myself this one question. “How much of these investments change how children approach education?” After being in the trenches for 15 years, building schools, designing how children learn, and training teachers from scratch, I've realized that very little capital goes into bringing real impact in education. Rebuilding what's taught, how it's taught, the curriculum, and teacher development is not where all of this capital goes. Whenever I evaluate education startups, I find the gap between what gets funded and what classrooms actually need to be staggering. Education never had a funding problem. It had a patience problem. Real change in education takes decades to build. It involves rewiring how teachers think and redesigning assessment. It involves convincing a parent that their child should learn design thinking. Education should be fun for children while also empowering them for the future. It is slow and unglamorous, but it works. The founders and investors who treat education like the complex, human, deeply difficult system it is are the ones who'll actually make a difference. The best thing that could happen to Indian education is capital that understands what classrooms actually need. P.S. If you're building in education, what's one thing you wish investors understood better?

    *   ![Image 34: No alternative text description for this image](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)

[![Image 36: View profile for Ramesh Muthuvel](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/ramesh-muthuvel-29674189?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Ramesh Muthuvel](https://in.linkedin.com/in/ramesh-muthuvel-29674189?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
PayPe Technologies Pvt Ltd•5K followers

 3d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Framesh-muthuvel-29674189_edtechindia-futureofeducation-educationreform-activity-7448361471127871488-s5v8&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

📚 “India invests millions in EdTech… But how much of it truly changes learning?” Every year, millions of dollars are invested in India’s education technology sector. 💰 But one important question remains 👉 Is this money truly transforming the way children learn? ✔️ Schools are being built ✔️ Apps are being created ✔️ Funding is flowing ❌ But is the method of teaching really changing? Are teachers being upskilled? Is the curriculum truly preparing students for the real world? 💡 The truth is: Education doesn’t have a funding problem. It has a patience problem. ⏳ Real transformation: • Won’t come from just an app • Won’t come from a single funding round It comes from 👇 👩🏫 A shift in teacher mindset 📖 Thoughtful curriculum redesign 🧠 Understanding how children actually learn 🚀 Education should: ✔️ Be joyful ✔️ Strengthen life skills It is slow. It’s not glamorous. But that’s where the real impact lies. 💯 🔥 The future of Indian education will not be built by funding… 👉 It will be built by understanding classrooms. [#EdTechIndia](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedtechindia&trk=public_post-text)[#FutureOfEducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofeducation&trk=public_post-text)[#EducationReform](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducationreform&trk=public_post-text)[#LearningMatters](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flearningmatters&trk=public_post-text)[#ClassroomImpact](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclassroomimpact&trk=public_post-text)[#TeacherDevelopment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fteacherdevelopment&trk=public_post-text)[#CurriculumDesign](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcurriculumdesign&trk=public_post-text)[#StudentCentric](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstudentcentric&trk=public_post-text)[#RealLearning](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Freallearning&trk=public_post-text)[#EducationInnovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducationinnovation&trk=public_post-text)[#IndiaEducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findiaeducation&trk=public_post-text)[#DigitalLearning](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdigitallearning&trk=public_post-text)[#BeyondFunding](h [](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/aksir_wiswits-edtech-edutech-activity-7439366076582457344-Q-e3)

[![Image 39: View profile for Anil Kumar](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/aksir?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Anil Kumar](https://in.linkedin.com/in/aksir?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
WisWits EduTech Pvt. Ltd.•5K followers

 4w    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Faksir_wiswits-edtech-edutech-activity-7439366076582457344-Q-e3&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Why Build Something New in EdTech… When Many Are Struggling? A fair question. Over the last few years, we have seen massive funding, rapid expansion, and equally rapid corrections in the EdTech industry. But one thing has never changed: India’s need for quality education is bigger than ever. Millions of students. Thousands of schools. And still a large gap between potential and guidance. After spending years in classrooms, mentoring students, and understanding the academic ecosystem deeply, I realized something important: The real opportunity in education is not in noise, hype, or aggressive scaling. It is in clarity of purpose. Strong academic foundations. And building with patience. For the past few months, I have been quietly working on something. No announcements yet. No big marketing campaigns. Just careful thinking, deep planning, and building with intent. Because sometimes the most meaningful things in education are built silently before they are seen. All I will say for now is this: Something interesting is coming. And when it arrives, it will be built with one clear goal — to genuinely improve academic outcomes for students and institutions. The journey is just beginning. Stay tuned. - Anil Kumar (AK Sir) Founder | Educator [WisWits EduTech Pvt. Ltd.](https://in.linkedin.com/company/wiswits?trk=public_post-text)[#wiswits](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fwiswits&trk=public_post-text)[#edtech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedtech&trk=public_post-text)[#edutech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedutech&trk=public_post-text)[#technology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftechnology&trk=public_post-text)[#education](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducation&trk=public_post-text)[#acdemics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Facdemics&trk=public_post-text)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521_schools-education-edtech-activity-7446733255619559424-vXlG)

[![Image 41: View profile for RAJENDRA PRASAD (RP Sir)](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/rajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[RAJENDRA PRASAD (RP Sir)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/rajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
SMART MODERN SENIOR SECONDARY…•5K followers

 1w    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521_schools-education-edtech-activity-7446733255619559424-vXlG&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

EDUCATION IS NO LONGER JUST A SERVICE. IT’S BECOMING A ₹25 LAKH CRORE INDUSTRY. Are we ready for what this means? India’s school education is transforming—fast. $117B → $313B by 2030 This is not growth. This is industrial-scale transformation. WHY IS THIS HAPPENING? Because everything changed: Aspirations ↑ Expectations ↑ Exposure ↑ Parents don’t want schooling anymore. They want outcomes. THE REAL DRIVER? Not policy. Not schools. 👉 Parents. They are demanding: English fluency Global readiness Career security And the market is responding. FOLLOW THE MONEY. Private Equity entering schools EdTech attracting billions School chains expanding aggressively 👉 Education is now an investment asset class SCHOOLS ARE CHANGING. From: ➡️ Independent institutions To: ➡️ Organized brands ➡️ Scalable systems ➡️ Replicable models Schools are becoming networks, not buildings ARE SCHOOLS INCREASING? Not just in number. In structure. More private schools More premium schools More “branded schooling” 👉 The shift is from quantity → organized quality IS THIS GOOD? Depends on who you ask. 👍 Better infrastructure 👍 More opportunities 👍 Global exposure ⚠️ Higher fees ⚠️ Commercialization ⚠️ Growing inequality 👉 Progress comes with a price. WHAT ABOUT TEACHERS? This is a turning point. From: ➡️ Teaching To: ➡️ Performance + accountability + data 👉 Teachers are becoming instructional professionals AND PRINCIPALS? No longer administrators. They are now: Strategic leaders Culture builders System designers 👉 The role is shifting towards a CEO mindset EDTECH IS NOT OPTIONAL ANYMORE. AI-driven learning Real-time assessment Personalized pathways 👉 Technology is not supporting education. It is reshaping it. IMPACT ON CHILDREN More exposure. More opportunities. But also: More pressure More structure Less breathing space 👉 Are we building learners… or performers? THE BIG QUESTION Is education still a mission? Or has it become a market? THE FUTURE WILL BELONG TO THOSE WHO CAN BALANCE BOTH. Scale + Soul Systems + Sensitivity Performance + Purpose 👉 Because education cannot be just business. And it cannot survive as just idealism. Education in India is entering a defining decade. We are witnessing the rise of: Organized school systems Investor-driven growth Technology-led transformation But the real question is not how fast we grow. It is: What kind of education are we building? For discussion (Give your valuable comments please): 👉 Is this shift strengthening education… or diluting its purpose? [#Schools](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fschools&trk=public_post-text)[#Education](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducation&trk=public_post-text)[#Edtech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedtech&trk=public_post-text)[#Principals](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fprincipals&trk=public_post-text)[#Teachers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fteachers&trk=public_post-text)[#Parents](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fparents&trk=public_post-text)[#Investors](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finvestors&trk=public_post-text)[#Directors](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdirectors&trk=public_post-text)[#Industry](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findustry&trk=public_post-text)[#Experts](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fexperts&trk=public_post-text)[#NikhileshBhardwaz](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnikhileshbhardwaz&trk=public_post-text)[#BSNMurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbsnmurthy&trk=public_post-text)[#DhananjaySirur](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdhananjaysirur&trk=public_post-text)[#ArchanaGaba](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Farchanagaba&trk=public_post-text)[#SavitaLaxman](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsavitalaxman&trk=public_post-text)[#Bora](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbora&trk=public_post-text)[#SanthoshSubramanian](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsanthoshsubramanian&trk=public_post-text)[#AshokPandey](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fashokpandey&trk=public_post-text)[#JCSharma](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjcsharma&trk=public_post-text)[#PratimaMittal](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpratimamittal&trk=public_post-text)

    *   ![Image 42: No alternative text description for this image](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)View C2PA information ![Image 43](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)

[![Image 44](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)![Image 45](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)![Image 46](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV) 58](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521_schools-education-edtech-activity-7446733255619559424-vXlG&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)[4 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521_schools-education-edtech-activity-7446733255619559424-vXlG&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)

To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521_schools-education-edtech-activity-7446733255619559424-vXlG&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/drashishtripathi_educationtransformation-leadership-managementeducation-activity-7447252211748773889-pjIH)

[![Image 47: View profile for Dr. Ashish Tripathi](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/drashishtripathi?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Dr. Ashish Tripathi](https://in.linkedin.com/in/drashishtripathi?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
Pillai University•9K followers

 6d    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdrashishtripathi_educationtransformation-leadership-managementeducation-activity-7447252211748773889-pjIH&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

This is one of the most important questions in education today — and it deserves a bold answer. 📚 India's education sector growing to ₹25 lakh crore isn't just a number. It's a signal that learning is no longer optional — it's a strategic investment. The shift from institutions to organized education brands, from administrators to CEO-mindset leaders, from teaching to technology-led transformation — this is exactly the disruption we're living through. At[Pillai University](https://in.linkedin.com/school/pillaiuniversity/?trk=public_post-text), we believe education must have both Scale AND Soul. We equip students not just with degrees, but with the leadership mindset, critical thinking, and real-world skills to thrive in this ₹25 lakh crore economy. Are we building learners or performers? At Pillai, we strive to build both. 👉 Read this post in full — it's a mirror for every educator and institution leader. Like, comment your perspective, and share it widely! [www.pillai.edu.in](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Epillai%2Eedu%2Ein&urlhash=RFPG&trk=public_post-text)[#EducationTransformation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducationtransformation&trk=public_post-text)[#Leadership](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fleadership&trk=public_post-text)[#ManagementEducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmanagementeducation&trk=public_post-text)[#IndiaEdTech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findiaedtech&trk=public_post-text)[#PillaiUniversity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpillaiuniversity&trk=public_post-text)[#HigherEducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhighereducation&trk=public_post-text)[#FutureOfEducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofeducation&trk=public_post-text)

[![Image 48: View profile for RAJENDRA PRASAD (RP Sir)](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/rajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[RAJENDRA PRASAD (RP Sir)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/rajendra-prasad-rp-sir-67672521?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

FOUNDER PRINCIPAL | PRESIDENT OF PSPA | IIM CALCUTTA-ELP, IIT DELHI - MIP

 1w 

EDUCATION IS NO LONGER JUST A SERVICE. IT’S BECOMING A ₹25 LAKH CRORE INDUSTRY. Are we ready for what this means? India’s school education is transforming—fast. $117B → $313B by 2030 This is not growth. This is industrial-scale transformation. WHY IS THIS HAPPENING? Because everything changed: Aspirations ↑ Expectations ↑ Exposure ↑ Parents don’t want schooling anymore. They want outcomes. THE REAL DRIVER? Not policy. Not schools. 👉 Parents. They are demanding: English fluency Global readiness Career security And the market is responding. FOLLOW THE MONEY. Private Equity entering schools EdTech attracting billions School chains expanding aggressively 👉 Education is now an investment asset class SCHOOLS ARE CHANGING. From: ➡️ Independent institutions To: ➡️ Organized brands ➡️ Scalable systems ➡️ Replicable models Schools are becoming networks, not buildings ARE SCHOOLS INCREASING? Not just in number. In structure. More private schools More premium schools More “branded schooling” 👉 The shift is from quantity → organized quality IS THIS GOOD? Depends on who you ask. 👍 Better infrastructure 👍 More opportunities 👍 Global exposure ⚠️ Higher fees ⚠️ Commercialization ⚠️ Growing inequality 👉 Progress comes with a price. WHAT ABOUT TEACHERS? This is a turning point. From: ➡️ Teaching To: ➡️ Performance + accountability + data 👉 Teachers are becoming instructional professionals AND PRINCIPALS? No longer administrators. They are now: Strategic leaders Culture builders System designers 👉 The role is shifting towards a CEO mindset EDTECH IS NOT OPTIONAL ANYMORE. AI-driven learning Real-time assessment Personalized pathways 👉 Technology is not supporting education. It is reshaping it. IMPACT ON CHILDREN More exposure. More opportunities. But also: More pressure More structure Less breathing space 👉 Are we building learners… or performers? THE BIG QUESTION Is education still a mission? Or has it become a market? THE FUTURE WILL BELONG TO THOSE WHO CAN BALANCE BOTH. Scale + Soul Systems + Sensitivity Performance + Purpose 👉 Because education cannot be just business. And it cannot survive as just idealism. Education in India is entering a defining decade. We are witnessing the rise of: Organized school systems Investor-driven growth Technology-led transformation But the real question is not how fast we grow. It is: What kind of education are we building? For discussion (Give your valuable comments please): 👉 Is this shift strengthening education… or diluting its purpose? [#Schools](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fschools&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Education](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feducation&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Edtech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedtech&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Principals](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fprincipals&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Teachers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fteachers&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Parents](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fparents&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Investors](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finvestors&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Directors](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdirectors&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Industry](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findustry&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Experts](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fexperts&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#NikhileshBhardwaz](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnikhileshbhardwaz&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#BSNMurthy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbsnmurthy&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#DhananjaySirur](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdhananjaysirur&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#ArchanaGaba](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Farchanagaba&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#SavitaLaxman](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsavitalaxman&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#Bora](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbora&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#SanthoshSubramanian](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsanthoshsubramanian&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#AshokPandey](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fashokpandey&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#JCSharma](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjcsharma&trk=public_post_reshare-text)[#PratimaMittal](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpratimamittal&trk=public_post_reshare-text)

To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdrashishtripathi_educationtransformation-leadership-managementeducation-activity-7447252211748773889-pjIH&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

*   
[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/subash-sankar-phd_highereducation-edtech-policymaking-activity-7438598423827697664-gKMj)

[![Image 52: View profile for Subash Sankar](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)](https://in.linkedin.com/in/subash-sankar-phd?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)[Subash Sankar](https://in.linkedin.com/in/subash-sankar-phd?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 
Dhanalakshmi Srinivasan…•557 followers

 1mo    

    *   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsubash-sankar-phd_highereducation-edtech-policymaking-activity-7438598423827697664-gKMj&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

We prohibit profit in higher education — but allow billion-dollar EdTech companies to sell learning. For decades, policy thinking has been shaped by the Supreme Court judgment in Unni Krishnan, J.P. v. State of Andhra Pradesh, which argued that education should not function as a commercial enterprise for profit. At face value, that principle sounds morally sound. But the reality around us tells a different story. Just a few months ago, several institutions across states were investigated under the PMLA for alleged financial irregularities. Meanwhile, EdTech companies — essentially technology-driven coaching ecosystems — attract venture capital and seed funding because they operate within a startup framework where education can be commercial. This creates a clear contradiction: • EdTech can raise venture capital. • Coaching ecosystems can operate as businesses. • But mainstream higher education institutions remain outside the startup ecosystem. The consequence is structural. Many capable academics cannot build institutions because they lack capital and cannot navigate regulatory red tape. Innovation in higher education often becomes limited to those who already possess financial power or institutional influence. This debate may soon become unavoidable. The Supreme Court has constituted a 9-judge bench to reconsider how sectors such as education, healthcare, and social enterprises should be interpreted in relation to profit and regulation. India has one of the largest youth populations in the world. A limited number of institutions like the Indian Institutes of Technology and National Institutes of Technology cannot shoulder that responsibility alone. If innovation and quality are serious priorities, perhaps the conversation should move toward legally recognised and ethically regulated educational entrepreneurship. Should higher education institutions be allowed to operate within a regulated startup ecosystem — or should profit remain outside mainstream education? Article on ED Raids under PMLA: [https://lnkd.in/gpdn7F92](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fgpdn7F92&urlhash=Dzt0&trk=public_post-text)[#highereducation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhighereducation&trk=public_post-text)[#edtech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fedtech&trk=public_post-text)[#policymaking](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpolicymaking&trk=public_post-text)[#innovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finnovation&trk=public_post-text)[#indianconstitution](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Findianconstitution&trk=public_post-text)

    *   ![Image 53: No alternative text description for this image](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tristha_every-year-many-millions-are-invested-in-activity-7447969062376091648-oabV)
